creative_brief: Hero image: close-up of clear-labelled meat packaging showing farm name, region, and date — sitting on a clean timber counter. Muted, honest tones — no glossy food styling. Below the fold, a simple illustrated supply chain diagram: farm → hub → hands. Photography should feel documentary, not aspirational.
headline: You don't know what's in your mince. That's by design.
subhead: Supermarkets strip the story from your food — where it grew, how it was raised, how far it travelled. FarmThru is building a grocery store that puts every answer back on the label.
hero_copy:
Pick up a pack of beef mince from the big chains. Try to find the farm name. The paddock practice. The distance it travelled. You won't. That information disappeared somewhere along a supply chain designed to move volume, not build trust.

FarmThru is a farm-direct grocery store. We partner with NSW farms and suppliers — roughly 15 to 20 right now — and move their food through one short chain: farm to hub to you. No unnecessary middlemen. No mystery.

We're opening our equity crowdfunding campaign on Birchal. You can own a piece of what we're building. But first, we want to show you exactly what that means — and what it doesn't.

sections.heading: The transparency problem isn't accidental
sections.body:
Supermarkets aggregate from hundreds of suppliers. Products get relabelled, repackaged, and shuffled through warehouses and distribution centres. By the time food reaches the shelf, the origin story is gone.

That's not a bug. Opacity lets margins hide. It lets quality slide without anyone noticing. It lets a pack of chicken sit under a house brand with no farmer's name in sight.

We think you deserve to know where your food comes from. Not as a marketing slogan — as a verifiable fact on every product you collect from our hub.

sections.heading: How our model keeps the chain short and visible
sections.body:
FarmThru runs a hub-and-collect model. You order online. You collect from our Brookvale hub — Unit 23, 10-18 Orchard Rd — Monday to Friday. We also offer delivery across Sydney, Central Coast, and Wollongong.

No subscriptions. No lock-in. No membership fees. You order when you want, and you buy in bulk to save — rewarding your commitment with real affordability.

Our partner farms and suppliers are across NSW. Around 15 to 20 identified on our site right now. Each one traceable. Each one accountable. That short chain — farm to hub to your hands — is what makes transparency possible. Not promises. Infrastructure.

sections.heading: Own a piece of it — with your eyes open
sections.body:
We're raising through equity crowdfunding on Birchal. Minimum investment is $50. Maximum is $10,000 per investor.

We want partners who believe food transparency matters enough to build a business around it. But we won't dress this up. FarmThru is early-stage. Investing in early-stage businesses carries real risk — you could lose some or all of your investment. There are no guaranteed returns.

We believe the risk and the mission deserve equal honesty. If that resonates, join the VIP waitlist with a $5 refundable deposit. You'll get first access to the campaign, priority updates, and an exclusive Q&A before we open to the public.

sections.heading: No commitment. Just clarity.
sections.body:
The $5 VIP deposit is fully refundable. It holds your spot and gets you priority access — nothing more, nothing less. You can walk away at any point.

If you just want groceries, no investment required. Order when you want. Collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday. Our tagline is simple: we'll show you where your food comes from.

But if you want to be part of building a grocery store where every product carries a provenance you can verify — where safety comes from transparency, not trust — then we'd like you on the inside early.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign will be hosted on Birchal. Investing in early-stage businesses carries risk, including the risk of losing your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. This is not financial advice. You should read the offer document and consider whether the investment is right for you before making any decision. Any investment decision is made at your own risk.
